title: Formal Complaint Against Comfort Wear
body:
Formal Complaint Against Comfort Wear – Deceptive Practices and Customer Mistreatment

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to file a formal complaint against Comfort Wear for deceptive business practices, refusal to honor refund requests, and unacceptable customer service.

I purchased two pairs of shoes totaling over $100.00. Both arrived in the wrong size. When I contacted Comfort Wear to request a refund and return the items, I was told — without being given any choice — to “keep them” and that I would receive a certificate for another pair. I explicitly rejected this offer and demanded a refund. The representative falsely claimed I had agreed to the certificate and refused to process a return.

Despite my repeated objections, Comfort Wear sent another pair of shoes — again in the wrong size — without my consent. I contacted them again to demand a refund, and not only did they refuse, but they also blocked me from further communication.

This behavior is unethical and violates basic consumer rights. Comfort Wear:

Sent incorrect products multiple times

Refused to issue a refund or accept returns

Misrepresented my statements

Took further action without my consent

Blocked me from contacting them

I am requesting immediate intervention to resolve this matter. I demand a full refund and accountability for the mistreatment I’ve experienced. If this issue is not resolved promptly, I will pursue further action through consumer protection channels and public reviews.

title: Poor product and dishonest marketing
body:
Poor product and dishonest marketing. The company does not honor its "money back guarantee."

I contacted the company shortly after receiving my shoes as they did not fit. They initially asked me to continue to wear them for 45 days to see if they got better (even though their website says they must be in new condition in order to return). I contacted them again a few days later, and was offered a 20% refund. I contacted them again and was offered a gift card in lieu of refund. Finally, they told me that I'd have to find someone (in person) to donate them so I could provide proof of donation. Only then would they start the refund process. There's nowhere like that near me--believe me, I've checked--so here I am with shoes that don't fit and out $60.

title: Scam Company
body:

This is by far the WORST shopping experience I’ve ever had. First, I ordered shoes that came big due to their misleading sizing. When I asked to return them, they offered me a “free replacement.” I specifically chose the correct size for the replacement, but they sent me with 1.5 size bigger than what I asked for. Now they are completely ignoring my messages and refusing to make things right.

This company is unprofessional, dishonest, and does not care about its customers. They lure you in with promises and then disappear when they make a mistake. Save yourself the frustration—DO NOT order from them!

title: I’m sharing my experience with Comfort…
body:
I’m sharing my experience with Comfort Ortho Wear to help others avoid the pain and frustration I went through.

I purchased two pairs of work shoes on September 15, 2025. Both were size 38, but neither fit properly. One pair caused immediate pain and was completely unusable. The second pair lacked traction — I slipped on a wet floor, risking serious injury. After brief use, I developed painful calluses and ongoing leg pain, something I’ve never experienced before despite wearing sneakers daily for work.

I contacted the company on September 23 and spoke with a representative named Gael. I explained everything: the discomfort, the safety risk, and the physical damage. Despite this, they refused to issue a full refund. First they offered 30%, then a gift card or a free pair — none of which addressed the real issue. I declined all alternatives and requested a full refund and return label.

My husband also contacted them, demanding resolution. The company continued to delay and refused to honor our request. Meanwhile, the shoes have remained packaged and ready for return since September 23.

I’ve filed formal complaints with the Better Business Bureau and the Federal Trade Commission, and initiated a credit card dispute. This company ignored legitimate concerns and refused to take responsibility for unsafe products.

I strongly advise others to think twice before buying from Comfort Ortho Wear. Their customer service is evasive, and their products caused physical harm. Consumers deserve better.

They wouldn't accept the shoes back due to hygiene reasons, but they had t even been worn. They then offered a gift card in the amount of the shoes, but never sent it. body: DO NOT BUY! If I could give negative stars I would. I bought a pair of shoes and they were way too big. I tried to return them and they said you should try to wear them for 45 days and break them in. I explained they were so large my foot falls out. They then offered 30% of my purchase price… I said no. I simply wanted to exchange for a smaller size or return. They responded with, I’m sorry you don’t qualify for a free pair. Poor!

title: Horrible quality shoes and terrible customer service!!
body: I purchased a pair of shoes from Comfort Wear in June of this year. I put them on and immediately knew that they wouldn’t work for me, as my toe felt to close to the front. I immediately contacted their customer service and explained that I wanted to return the shoes. They said I needed to keep them for 45 days and give them a chance. I wore them to work and was in excruciating pain, I explained this to Jesse, the customer service rep. She said that all they could do was either send a refund of 20% and keep the shoes or they could send a credit for $53. And order a different style. I chose the second option. Well they arrived and were too small. I ordered a 9 1/2, which is a half size larger than I usually wear. Again I contacted Jesse and was told that they could not do anything because they had already gone above and beyond in assisting me….really?? I just wanted to exchange the shoes for a larger size. This company just wants to sell the shoes, they don’t care if you are satisfied or not!! I have never had a company not stand by their product before…and I have ordered a lot of items online. I would NEVER buy from this company again!! Shoes feel cheaply made too.

body:

$85 lesson on my part.
Steve

title: DO NOT BUY FROM THIS COMPANY!
body:

Then nothing - I have have sent loads of emails and no reply at all. Clearly a SCAM with no intention of refunding or exchanging.

title: A DISHONEST COMPANY WHO DOES NOT STAND BEHIND THEIR PRODUCTS OR POLICIES
body:
July 21, 2025 -----I bought a pair of shoes from ComfortWear online. BIG HUGE MISTAKE!!! They claim their shoes are "Ortho wear with orthotic support". The shoes I got looked good and were pretty, but there was NO ORTHOTIC SUPPORT. I tried them on AND I wore socks for hygiene purposes. Once I put them on, I found I was walking on my left ankle. My left foot pronates inward. I have to have actual arch support for my flat feet. Plus they were extremely hard to get on. I am 69 years old and have arthritis in my hands so that made it even more difficult.

I contacted the company 3 times within 2 weeks of my purchase (7/28-8/5/25). Each time they came up with an excuse for me not to return them. Then in the final communication they said they could not accept them back for "hygienic reasons and the safety of their employees". These shoes are in their original condition...NEW! I tried them on ONCE WEARING SOCKS! Then I put them back in the plastic bag they came in.

THEIR 45 DAY RETURN POLICY IS A LIE!! Their website states "Feel the difference in 45 days, or get 100% of your money back." And it also states "Free Returns". ALL OF THAT IS FALSE ADVERTISING!! 🧐🤨😠😡 They WILL NOT LET YOU RETURN THE SHOES! A company that does NOT stand behind their products or policies is NOT a company that can be trusted. Their Trustpilot score should be zero!!! They are outright liars.

STEER CLEAR OF THIS COMPANY IF YOU KNOW WHAT'S GOOD FOR YOU. SAVE YOUR MONEY AND GO ELSEWHERE!!

I WOULD HAVE GIVEN THEM ZERO STARS IF IT WAS AVAILABLE!!!

title: ComfortWear refused to give me a refund.
body:
The shoes lack cushioning at the front part of the shoe while lifting up at the heel, thus putting more pressure on the ball of the foot. It is not a comfortable shoe. When I asked to return the shoes and receive a refund, I received the run- around. First, they wanted me to wear the shoes for a while, which would have negated a full refund as they would have been used. Then, they offered only 20% refund only. When I refused that, they countered with the offer of me donating the shoes to Goodwill, presenting the receipt to them, then I would receive a lesser refund.
It is totally unacceptable.

title: AVOID AT ALL COST MAJOR SCAM company
body:
AVOID AT ALL COST MAJOR SCAM company. I would give this a negative 1000 million if I could. This is the nightmare you will deal with trying to get your money back. This is email #4 response with me trying to get my money back after the first day of receiving these shoes I notified them asking for a return and on their website it says 45 day moneyback guarantee, but this is the bull crap I have been dealing with in trying to get my refund.
They are not part of the Better Business Bureau and they know they have you. So plan on these shoes being crap and the positive reviews are BS and you can just kiss whatever money you pay for these shoes goodbye because you are about to go on a major jacked up runaround trip. Here is the response to the last email they sent me.

Hi Valerie,

Thanks for getting back to me.

It makes me sad that you don't want a free pair offer.

We genuinely aim to ensure your satisfaction and extend a gesture of goodwill. Nevertheless, I respect your decision and would like to assist you in resolving this matter promptly.

To facilitate your refund and ensure the shoes find a purposeful use, I suggest returning them to one of the donation centers operated by The Salvation Army. This organization provides vital support to those in need, and your contribution can make a significant difference.

You can easily locate your nearest Salvation Army Thrift Store by entering your zip code on their website: (they listed the website link to the Salvation Army,but trust pilot does not allow this so I removed it)

However, if getting to a Salvation Army center is difficult, you also have the option to donate to a local donation center. Simply provide proof of donation, such as the donation slip they provide, for a refund.

After you have donated your shoe, please provide proof of donation with the donation slip they provide. This will allow us to expedite the refund process for you promptly.

Thank you for your support and have a wonderful day!

Gael, Customer Service
ComfortWear

title: I give them a 1 because I at least…
body: I give them a 1 because I at least received the shoes, but they are too big, I purchased size guarantee, so have paid for the return in advance, however after 4 emails over 2 weeks withe no response from the customer service team. I will not purchase from them again. The shoes feel very cheap in quality.

title: Trouble returning shoes
body: I had heard good things about these shoes but the fit is not good for me. Not good arch support and too wide. I have been trying to talk to customer support team and am having poor experience with them. They do not seem to want to give a refund despite their claim of free returns. Plus they are not getting back to me despite promise of return contact within 24 hrs.

title: Wasn't aware they were coming from…
body: Wasn't aware they were coming from another country. They still haven't arrived, which means they are late. Had I known they were coming from another country, I would have expected them to be awhile. However since you don't tell customers when they are ordering, it will keep me from ordering again.

title: Difficult to work with on returns and refunds
body: I bought two pairs of shoes recently - sandals and sneakers. They day I got them, I tried one of each on and immediately knew that they wouldn't work for me. I emailed customer service the same day to start the return process - WELL within their 45-day from receiving policy. First email they tried to talk me into just trying them out for 45 days, that new shoes needed to be "broken in". I emailed back stating that wasn't the issue. My toes were hanging off the top of the sandals, and the sneaker's sole felt unsteady on my foot, like I'd twist my ankle. They then offered me 30% back to keep them anyhow, stating that returning the shoes, if they didn't pass inspection?? They would be thrown away. Umm, why?? I literally had one on my foott for less than a minute and immediately put it back in its original packaging, which I explained. Again they wrote back offering me a different pair at no cost or a gift card, saying that even if the shoes were in good condition, they'd likely be thrown away and they're trying to minimize material waste, and something about for the health and safety of their employees??? So - do they NEVER accept returns? That's the way I'm reading it. People return things all the time. To state something like this is absolutely ludicrous, to me. Beside the fact that - NO! I don't like the shoes. I want my money back! At this point, I really feel like I won't be getting my money back. The shoes are not well made, in my opinion, and definitely not worth the money. I want my money back. I don't think I've ever had this kind of difficulty trying to return something, especially when I am WELL within their policy to do so.

title: Horrible company!
body: I don’t know where the positive experiences came from. They’re probably fake. The purchase process was easy. But they are horribly uncomfortable. I tried return them, unworn. Forget it. After almost a dozen back and forth emails, they told me to donate them and send them the donation receipt and they would refund them. Sure they would. Obviously I didn’t jump through that hoop and just disputed the charge with my credit card company.

title: Dont buy from them
body: Waiting on reply to return ! They responded quick until.i told them.i wanted a full refund.im going to contact the better buisness beruea ill change my review if they refund me

title: Terrible customer service
body: I immediately contacted them when the shoes arrived and were too small. They asked me to send photos of the shoes and packaging which I did - they promised to send me details of how to send them back and re dive a full refund. STILL NO NEWS AND THEY DONT REPLY TO EMAILS.

title: I agree with Ann and Rebeca still…
body:
I agree with Ann and Rebeca still waiting to return product and get a refund its been 2 months now I guess NINA is keeping the money
Don't buy from this company unless you enjoy loosing money

title: Do not buy shoes from this company
body: Do not buy shoes from this company. I bought shoes true to size, I measured my foot. The shoes are over an inch too small. They are refusing to give me a refund. I even paid extra money for the shoe fit guarantee where I get a full refund,if the shoes do not fit. they are refusing to honor it. I've been emailing with them over a week. It's been horrible. Again do not buy from this company

body:
The quality of the shoes is very bad.
It is said free return and get back full refund but I have trouble of sending the the shoes back to them.
It can scan me once but not twice
Those who want to buy these better think twice.

title: These are garbage
body: These are garbage. No arch support and not the size they claim to be. I have persevered for over a month thinking they would stretch and be more comfortable, but they absolutely cripple me.

title: HORRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE
body: This is a REAL REVIEW. Ordered these shoes for $53.95, then the order was delayed - more than likely because they come from China - so they didn't arrive on time where I was temporarily located because I travel to a different location weekly. Luckily a friend was able to get them when they arrived 2 weeks later and I paid her to ship them to me. I was so looking forward to getting these shoes, and totally dissapointed when they were too narrow and 100% uncomfortable. Weeks later - it is now July 16, I am STILL trying to get a refund. They want to refund me $20!!!!! HORRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE!!!!! BEWARE!!!!!!

title: Not happy
body: I purchased two pairs of shoes one 8 1/2 and one a nine. I ordered them and it took over a month to receive them. The size 9 shoes were too small and I wanted to exchange or return and they told me their exchange return window is 30 days from when you order. Not acceptable it should be 30 days from when you receive them. Not happy at all. They gave me a 20% refund. That was $22 of a $112 order and I gave the shoes away.

title: These are some of the worst shoes I…
body:
These are some of the worst shoes I have ever bought!!! I bought them for my daily walks. After my first 2 mile walk, on a flat sidewalk, my feet had slipped forward so bad my toenail was bruised. I have hiked most of my adult life, and only other time this has happened was hiking the 7+ miles down the South Kaibab trail in the grand canyon.
The shoes are stiff and very uncomfortable. Talk about cheap junk.
They also took more than a month to ship. I didn't realize they were going to ship from, and are made in China, or I wouldn't have bought them.

title: Do NOT buy!
body: Do NOT buy from this fraudulent company. The shoes are very poor quality and offer little to NO arch support. When you want a refund and to return the shoes they give you the runaround and will NOT issue a refund. This company should not be permitted to sell their product.

title: AVOID AVOID AVOID
body: Just got these shoes on Friday. I am a surgical tech and I’m on my feet all day. I ordered 81/2 in two different styles, one fits great the other is just a tad too small. But, I’m writing this review so people are aware of this…..most uncomfortable shoes I own. After only wearing them for two days, my feet hurt so bad that I had to hire someone to walk my dog for me since I can barely walk. The heel is a great deal higher than the ball of the foot therefore your foot slides up in the toe box. The add said good for plantar fasciitis….wrong. Not comfortable. AVOID THESE SHOES AT ALL COST. DO NOT BUY. TOTAL SCAM.

title: Don't buy shoes from this company
body:
Don't buy shoes from this company. I ordered 2 pairs. I gave one to my sister, she tried it and did not like the way the shoes fit. She asked me to return it. I tried mine and it was big. The shoes are super UNCOMFORTABLE. IT FEELS VERY WEIRD. The higher heel pushes the foot forward which rubs against the top of the shoes when walking.
After trying it out, I agreed with my sister and decided to return both shoes. I contacted the company and asked to return the shoes. They gave me a run around.
1. They asked me to take picture of the shoes.
2. Offered to send me a new pair. While I was deciding on a different style. They decided to send me a new pair without my consent. When I told customer support that I want to return both shoes due to how uncomfortable these are.
3. Customer support told me to take the shoes to donate to a local salvation army, then send them the receipt as proof. I did.
4. A day later after receiving my receipt, customer support informed me that a new shoes was on the way to my house and I would only receive refund for one shoes.
5. 2 weeks later, no refund is received. I contacted them and they told me no refund since they sent me a new pair and that's it. Case Closed.
Please be careful.
After I posted the review, I received an updated email from customer support informing me that a credit for one pair is refunded to my account.

title: This was/is the worse customer service…
body:
This was/is the worse customer service experience for me. As one reviewer commented, they give you a complete run around if you are requesting a refund. They refuse to give return instructions, then offer you a 30% discount to keep the shoes. When I refused, they offered a second pair or gift card instead. They claim retuning the shoes would harm their employees. Wow!!

The shoes simply did not fit. They requested photos initially, when I provided the photos, they wanted a different angle showing the size…I sent photos showing all, including the packaging and the soles showing the shoes had not been worn to no avail.

At the end of the day, I don’t want anything from them and will donate the shoes. If you are unsure of sizing do not order from them. They are the worst retailer I have ever dealt with online. If I can save you the trouble this post was worth it!

title: Dont do it! Scam!
body: These shoes were advertised as full refund ,wear for 45 days, will make sure fit correct. I ordered a 37 got a 38! They made we take a picture of size to prove they were wrong. Ridiculous! Then they offered a 30% refund Stated due to health concerns they dont do refund. Argued with them for a couple months. They sent another pair it was also the wrong size.

title: Sucky Customer Service-No help! I ordered a pair of the comfort wear…
body: I ordered a pair of the comfort wear shoes in a size 6.5, not being certain of the size I needed. I normally wear size 6, but decided to go 1/2 size larger to be sure. My shoes came and I immediately tried them on inside my house. I did not even walk in them because I knew right away they were a little short. I contacted customer service and asked if I could send back and exchange for the same shoe, just size 7 this time. I was given one answer after another, and all just a run around to use up my 45 day window to return or refund. No one would send a return label like they said they would, wouldn’t take the shoes back, then would take them back, and never would return my money nor send a replacement size. Their website even says 100% guarantee, easy return or refund. They lie! This is the very worst customer service I have ever seen! These people are scamming customers if they are claiming 100% guarantee, return or refund, but doing none of that! I have learned my lesson & will never order from them again. My advice: Do NOT order from this company unless you are absolutely sure of the size you need, as these shoes run SMALLER than you think & customer service sucks! They don’t care if they help you or not. They have your money and that’s all they care about!!

title: Returning the shoes
body: I bought a pair and when they arrived I could not wear them. They were not wide enough for my foot. I wanted to return them and I inquired but never received an answer on how to or where to send them back. I am out $60.00. Now it is passed the 30 day return policy,

title: Ordered size 8 1/2
body: Ordered size 8 1/2. Recieved size 5 1/2. Website days easy returns contact us for instructions. 5 emails later still No instructions. They tried telling me they prefer not to take the shoes back due to hygiene issues. All I wanted was a simple exchange for the correct size. Horrible experience! Buyer beware!

title: These shoes are not true to size
body: These shoes are not true to size. I ordered a 10 and a half and cannot get them on my feet. I requested a refund which they refused to to comply with there 45-day full refund policy. They offered 30% and then they offered store credit. I reported them to the Better Business Bureau. Strongly advise buying a different shoe

title: HORRIBLE customer service
body: HORRIBLE customer service. DO NOT BUY THESE SHOES!!! THEY WILL NOT REFUND YOUR MONEY!! I bought 2 pair. Tried one pair. Wore them for hours, long walks etc. They are just ok as far as comfort. BUT THEY ARE REALLY HARD TO PUT ON! Decided to return 2nd pair, never worn. I have been exchanging emails with "Myka" (real person? I doubt it) After 2 weeks and 12 emails, they want me to donate the shoes, provide proof, send it to them THEN they will issye partial refund. I doubt thst, too. PLEASE DO NOT BUY THESE!!!

title: These shoes are uncomfortable
body: These shoes are uncomfortable. I am not happy with the value of these shoes. Very cheaply made. They do not fit correctly.

title: I request an exchange for a smaller size, now a refund
body: I request an exchange for a smaller size, but I have to return first. I have to pay shipping to return and they will deduct shipping costs from my refund, which they never said how much that would be and never gave me the address to return the shoes. I sent a picture of the shoes, which were only worn once, but never heard back from them. They are waiting till their 45 return policy is up. Disgusting way to do business, 99.9% of companies have free shipping and send a prepaid label for returns.

title: DO NOT BUY FROM THIS COMPANY
body:
DO NOT BUY FROM THIS COMPANY.
The product is not good. It has zero support. Not good for anything.
They refuse to honor their return policy. Went so far as to lie to American Express to get my dispute overturned. I have cancelled my AMEX because of them. They asked me to donate the shoes after weeks of asking for the return instructions. I did and sent all photos and receipts. They then ghosted me. I am now out shoes, money and an AMEX card. Both companies can go bankrupt as far as I am concerned.

title: Not a good experience
body: I finally got my shoes after waiting for two weeks. Tried them on and they are way too big. I've been emailing them for over a week, but no one responds. Can't find a phone number to call either. Don't waste your money

title: Worst Return Process Ever
body: Bought shoes and my feet were hurting after an hour. Had to email back and forth for a week trying to return. They will offer you everything but your money. First it was wait for the 45 day guarantee then it was a 30% refund, then it was a full gift card. Then it was you can return but only to salvation army and provide the receipt. The absolute worst. They said no questions asked but asked a whole lot. The person responding to the emails had a horrible attitude. DONT DO IT!

title: I just purchased a pair of shoes that I…
body: I just purchased a pair of shoes that I didnt get!! I received a txt message that the shoes was delivered.. at around 1-4. I got off work excited at 730pm and when I came home no shoes so I check the tracking and it said delivered so it was a picture to prove delivery!! But when I looked at the pictures they freaking delivered to the wrong address and then I emailed them to let them know it says delivered but if you look at the pictures it is to the wrong address.. now comfort wear is trying lie and say I received them when I clearly did not!!

title: Do not deal with this horrible company!
body: The company is unethical and lies in its advertising. I ordered the shoes for a walking vacation, paying extra for quicker delivery. They were sent from China with no possible way of coming on time after clearing customs and shipping to the west coast. They took my "shipping" money anyway and delivered after I came back from the vacation. Do not pay for expedited shipping; you are throwing away your money. It might as well be a donation to their company. If you open the package to try on the shoes, they will not take it back because it is "not in the original packaging." If you try to ask your credit card for a chargeback, they threaten you with criminal prosecution. Stay away from this company.

title: Very cheaply made & cannot return
body: The shipping was extremely slow. Upon receipt of the shoes I wasn't happy at all with the purchase. The shoes seem very cheaply made. For the price I figured they would be made better. I don't think they will hold up at all. Upon reaching out immediately (day I received them) ask to return. Cannot return they must be donated to Salvation Army or donate store send copy of receipt to Comfortwear before refund can bring issued. More money & time out of my pocket. BUYER BEWARE

title: Poor products that don’t meet expectations. Refund ripoff!
body:
I just purchased these shoes, that just came last week. They have never touched the outside ground and my house floor for less than one minute. I tried them on,‘I
did not like the plastic or the fit! I emailed the supposedly Customer Service Support. Emailed me back they would hate to wast material and they would take 30% off off the amount paid. I hope they have a phone number and another person on the phone. I’ll be reporting them to the BBB and my States Attorney’s Office to investigate them, selling products that don’t meet expectations or meet medical guidelines or fit the support people expected. The fact that will not providing full refunds. I haven’t gotten a returned email, when I time him I’ll make sure the shoes looks 30% worn and send them back. I’ll take my 70% at that time.

title: Refund request ignored
body: Received my shoes; did not fir; emailed a return request that has been completely ignored. They have return guidelines posted that they are ignoring. Awful horrible company. Stay away. Filed a complaint with better business bureau. Hoping they can help.

title: Do yourself a favor and DON'T buy these shoes
body: The shoes came at a snail's pace. When they arrived and I tried them on, the first thing I noticed was the difficulty I had to get my foot into the shoe. After a half hour of struggling I was finally able to get into the shoe. I wore them in the house so they wouldn't get dirty in case I wanted to send them back. I contacted the company to tell them i was having problems and their response was that they were new and needed to be stretched out. I tried again but after wearing them 2 hours, my feet started to hurt. I contacted them again and was told that it would be a waste of material if I was to return them. They offered me a discount of 20% and told me to donate them to someone who needs them. They do not stand by their claims. There is no return and get your money back. I am sorry I ever did business with this company. Do yourself a favor and DON'T buy these shoes.

title: Sizing way off—too big
body:
Sizing way off—too big. Returns impossible! They play the whole “keep the item, we will credit you 30% game.

Finally I agreed to a gift card. Big surprise, the gift card did not work and my PayPal account was charged! Attempted to cancel immediately with no luck. They shipped my new order the next day. Totally unacceptable! Order from SAS. Made in the USA and no hassle returns or exchanges.

Order Number 1452880

I’m a pissed off senior!!!!!!!!

title: DO NOT BUY!!!
body: What a lie the shoe is on top of their 45 day money back guarantee 100%!! They scammed me by not guaranteeing the money back within two weeks of trying to shoe that did not help at all with foot pain. Then they were telling me if I ship it back that they have to inspect the shoe and I would only get a partial refund in the end they ended up making me drive to the ghetto to a Salvation Army donation location to donate the shoes and provide them the receipt which still does not guarantee 100% refund!!! on top of that I also have my credit card number stolen from this purchase!!!!

title: My order was sent to the wrong address…
body: My order was sent to the wrong address and was returned I am being told that they will not replace the order but will give me 30% off. What a rip off and scam.

title: This is a scam, be aware
body: I purchased the size shoe I normally wear according to their shoe chart. Their shoe size is one size short. I also purchased an insert for arch support and sent me support for heel. They said they would replace the shoe and never did, they also ignored the wrong inserts sent and offered S30.00 credit with shoe replacement for customer satisfaction and never did replace the inserts or shoes. I lost over $90.00 total with poor promises. People should band together who got taken from this company and report them to Better Business Bureau. They purposely send you the wrong items and scam you out of the total fees for the shoes.

title: DO NOT BUY FROM COMFORT FOOTWEAR
body: I bought 2 pairs of shoes from ComfortWear for starting on the wards. When they arrived they were uncomfortable, the sizing was strange and they were terrible quality, very cheaply made. No support whatsoever. The company states they offer free returns for refund or exchange. I contacted them to arrange the return for a refund. The first email back told me to take the insole out of the shoe and see if that makes it fit. I obviously wasn’t going to do this and kept requesting a return and refund. After multiple emails of them trying to convince me to keep the shoes, they eventually sent me a returns slip which was invalid when I tried to post it. I asked for another which they sent which was also invalid. They then told me to return the shoes to a valid Salvation Army store instead and then I could have my refund. They sent a link with their valid Salvation Army stores, all of which were only in America when I’m in Australia (I confirmed that the company do not exclude Australia from returns and refunds prior). Finally they told me they would send me a free international returns label. This was 2 weeks ago. I have contacted them multiple times and they are now ignoring all of my emails. I ended up having to request a refund for the $200 through my credit card provider. This company is impossible to deal with, terrible quality shoes and I’m now convinced a scam company based on the issues with the return and refund. I have been emailing them for 1 month now and the issue is still not resolved.

title: Terrible customer service
body: Terrible customer service, do not buy shoes from this company!!!

title: Awful!!
body: Awful!!! It's incredible that just the shoes are refundable but not the insoles!! And they just don't wanna get the shoes back!! Instead they make you donate them. That's not what the website says! One week using them and my feet are still killing me !!

title: Comfort wear shoe are made terrible
body:
Comfort wear shoe are made terrible, Read, Read, Read the return policy.
The shoes are very uncomfortable, I feel like it has a hard ball of Jello that moves around,on the Heal of the shoe, wore them for less than 1 hour I had crucial pain, on my feet. Cheap made, runs small in size. What caught my eye about these shoes they look so cute. Couldn't wait to put them on, big mistakes.

title: Terrible
body: Terrible, terrible, terrible. This brand sells the EXACT same shoes as Orthofit brand which means that not only are the statements about hand crafted by professional orthopedists are FALSE and DELIBERATE MISINFORMATION, but that these "shoes" are nothing than cheaply made dropshipped junk from a bunch of Chinese factory workers. Doesn't sound legit to me. Shame on this brand, I'm getting a refund on my money and returning the shoes one way or another whether that means going about it cordially or escalating it with my bank/the legal system (Better Business Bureau). I am that petty and I will go that far so choose wisely ComfortWear! F*** dropshippers from the bottom of my heart you guys suck! Make something useful of yourself and stop lying to drain the working class's pockets! Lazy bums...

title: This company totally scam and fraud i…
body: This company totally scam and fraud i don't even orderd shoes thay send me shoes thay charged me bill 121 $ I don't even placed the orderd nothe be careful about this website I calling them since 3 days thay don't even reply me nothing don't trust them

title: In starting to get a bad feeling…
body:
In starting to get a bad feeling already since they’ve been sitting in a warehouse for a week.
“Tracking” my ass.
Pretty sure it’s a scam. Very expensive one.
Save your money.

title: Haven't received my order yet
body:
I
Haven't received my order yet I guess it's been a week and half now. if I'm not going to get my shoes please refund my $52 and change back.. thank you

title: Don't trust the positive reviews!
body:
I received the shoes in a timely manner, but one of them was not made correctly, the sole leaned in. I wore them for a day and my body was in pain. When I reached out, I was told to try them again, I did... same issues. When I asked for my money to be refunded I have gotten nothing but run around - I am up to 10 email exchanges so far and the "best" offer they have presented is to keep the shoes (that I can't wear) and giving me 20% of my purchase price. (eight emails ago they offered me 30%) I had to take pictures of the shoes and size, they wanted me to donate them and send a receipt (I won't donate defective shoes). Their emails have always been prompt and polite, but despite the fact that I have been very clear in stating that the shoes are defective so I will not donate or give away, that I don't want another pair or a gift card, and that I want a full refund, they can't seem to get the message.
This has so frustrated me this is the first time that I have written a review EVER! PLEASE DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY!

6/13 Update - after even more emails - the last written by my husband saying that he will post about the negative experience on every social media account we have, I finally got a prepaid mailing address to return the shoes and my account has been credited.

title: Ordered my sneakers on April 27
body: Ordered my sneakers on April 27, 2025 wish I would’ve seen this review before nobody needs to order from this company. They’re not real sneakers. They’re cheap plastic things from China when you try and send them back they won’t give you a receipt or they won’t give you something to return it to them and they want you to take it and donate it to a donation center, and then provide the receipt to them and then they’ll give the money back so they can write it off as they donated it. It’s a scam stay away.

title: Took forever to get and won't give refund
body: Took forever to get them. Put them on, walked 2 house away from me had to return back home. They hurt my planters fasciitis and also when I walked my toes slid to the top of the shoe. Everytime I send email, they want to offer a 30% back and keep the shoe or get a free pair, nope. I contacted PayPal (thank God I use PayPal to purchase anything) to do a dispute because it says 45 day money back return. all they do is keep offering something different so naturally the 45 days is almost up. PayPal gave them until June 1st to contact them. If not, then PayPal will refund my money then go after comgortorthowear.

title: The shoes look good but are sloppily…
body: The shoes look good but are sloppily made. I ordered athletic shoes and they looked stretched out and had no side support. I wanted to return them but the company convinced me to get another pair free. I picked a sandal. The straps do not fit into the buckles. The chemical smell was so strong! I put them in the garage to see if they would ever work. The smell is almost gone but the straps do not fit into the buckle. I don't know if they are comfortable because nothing fit. The workmanship is terrible.

title: Delivery was great
body:
Delivery was great, color not quite the same as the picture, Quality was ok, but feel I could have bought the same thing with better quality for the same price at Softmoc (a store in Canada) Overall they are ok and I will wear them.

Ok - update
Now that I have worn them- They are the hardest shoes to put on. that little flap that looks like you are wearing socks always folds down into the shoe and you have to dig it out because it is uncomfortable. Not the most comfortable to wear and I would not recommend these shoes. Go to a store and try shoes on. don't waste your money on theses

title: Not a Fan
body: It took a long time to get my shoes, they have a STRONG chemical smell, NO support & No cushioning. I am an average size 7 women's, the size fit good, a little loose fitting so no support there. The style is cute....but they are nowhere near what they promised!!

title: A total waste of money - no money back
body: A total waste of money. As someone with foot issues, I am always on the hunt for a good shoe. I get tired of wearing tennis shoe style, but it does seem to be my lot in life, so was excited to find a shoe that had a little bit of a different look at least. I ordered directly from their website vs amazon or any one of the multitude of social media resellers. I paid, with everything about 60ish dollars. After quite a while, a couple of weeks, i reached out to them because i had received nothing. the payment had gone through, but no "hey thanks for ordering" no "we got your order" nothing, zip. So I honestly kind of forgot about them, until i saw the charge on my card, and was like hey...where are those, it had been a couple of weeks. so i reached out, but honestly at this point, figured i had been scammed. To my surprise, they got back to me really fast, assured me that the item had shipped and just so happened to be arriving the next day. Great. I open the box, and they are cute, have a slightly different style to them, and my initial try on was like, NICE! This feeling would fade. So, I will say this, if you have foot issues, or foot swelling issues, where you find it difficult to wear anything that doesn't lace up, you might actually be really happy with this shoe. I rarely find a slip on i can slide into. so we are 2 for 2. I decide to wear them the next day. now rule of thumb is you never wear new shoes on days you are going to be on your feet all day, so this was calculated. My day was office focused, with very little 'running' around. Oh thank heavens for that, because my feet were killing me an hour in. By the end of the day, I wanted to burn them. I have worn them once since, and it was for a short couple of hours to dinner because they matched my outfit. literally walked from the house to the car, to my friends front door, and into the restaurant. I couldn't wait to get them off when i got home. There is zero padding in the bottom of these shoes. So, I reached out, because they did claim to have a 100% satisfaction guarantee. crickets, nothing. life is too short, i don't really have 60ish bucks to throw away, but I also do not have the energy to keep at it, so i will warn you here. If you like them great, but if you counting on that money back guarantee, pay more and buy them from amazon, at least you can return them...i think, double check before you hit buy. I tried to do the right thing and order straight from them, cutting out the extra fees, and well, figuratively shot myself in the foot.

title: Disappointing
body: Bought these for my holiday and decided to give them a trial run. Nice looking shoe but when I started walking there was no arch support so my foot moved around in the shoe. Halfway through the walk I noticed I was getting a large blister on the underside of my heel because the soles are quite hard. All in all a nice looking shoe but not comfortable, I’ll have to put insoles in to give support and cushioning . Disappointing.

title: Prompt in sending
body: Prompt in sending, and taking your money, very bad customer service when needing an exchange or refund.

title: Good quality but very uncomfortable.I
body: Good quality, shoe, but very uncomfortable.I am nurse on my feet for twelve hours and my feet hurt for 2 days after wearing these shoes... And furthermore, try to return and it says I have to have the original packaging which was nothing but a plastic bag taped a 1000 times that had to be cut open. So of course, it went straight to the trash would not recommend this company.

title: They are light
body: They are light. But I have to tighten the shoes a lot to keep my feet from slipping and sliding around in the shoe. Once tight they still slide around and the plastic peace hurts the top of my foot. I has such high hopes.

title: I was very excited to try these shoes
body: I was very excited to try these shoes. I love the room in the toes, and my foot is floppy in the whole shoe to the point of sliding forward and hitting the front of the shoe. I think I am going to have to return them. I've tried cinching the laces and wearing thicker socks to no avail.

title: The shoes look cute BUT…
body: The shoes look cute, and do feel comfortable with no need to break them in. But, that’s probably because the entire top portion is so soft and pliable that it offers almost zero support to the ankles and feel very weak and just not well made. The thick sole is very nice the shoe really is roomy for the foot, however I wish the top was made with more supportive materials and that the ankles would feel secure. I also had to add a new insole for plantar fasciitis, without that it really wouldn’t even work for that. My heal pain is still just as bad as before I got these shoes :(

title: Non-existent customer service
body: Of the two pair of shoes I ordered, both were too big. One will work with replacing the flimsy insole with a better one. The other just won’t work, even though it’s the same size. Total inconsistency. I can’t get any help returning them. Every day for over a week, they do not answer the emails, texts merely refer you to email, as does the phone. Non-existent customer service for returns.

title: The shoes arrived and they are really…
body: The shoes arrived and they are really loose uppers (not adjustable) and the soles are hard and uncomfortable. The reason for the 2 stars so far is because when I contacted them their contact email doesn't work. If I can find how to contact them and they agree to the free return they showed when I bought them, I'll revise my opinion.

title: ORDER ONLY IF YOU HAVE LOADS OF TIME...LONG DELIVERY TIME
body: I suppose we are all spoiled by "microwave service". I am growing weary of waiting on my shoes! Ordered on 6/12. Latest tracking shows items have cleared "customs", which tells me they shipped via SLOW BOAT from somewhere. I just want my shoes!! Heaven forbid they don't fit & I need to exchange. CS and delivery time need massive improvement!

title: Not worth the hype!
body:
I recently purchased a pair of Comfortwear shoes. They're okay for walking in them like if you're just taking a leisurely stroll. But I can't say that they are good for being on your feet all day or for even a few hours!
I managed to complete three shifts in them despite feeling I was standing on cinder blocks!
Also, I purchased in all black pair but ended up receiving the black and purple pair!

title: Not what I was hoping for
body: I find these shoes to be like wearing a sock with a thick rubber sole. The shoe does not support my foot and my foot moves around when I walk. They fit well but don’t walk well at all. I don’t like wearing them.

title: I’ve been trying to return them for…
body: I’ve been trying to return them for several months now nobody will even get back to me. It’s just ridiculous. The shoes did not fit properly so I just wanted to change them out and they’ve gone ghost on me.

title: Don't buy
body: The shoes came in a decent time. I bought them for. Vacations because we would be doing a decent amount of walking. They are uncomfortable and poor quality. They are not even 6 months old and are falling apart (top part of the shoe is separating from the bottom and the shoe laces no longer tighten), are noisy when walking and about a month ago I needed to put in shoe pads. I should have stayed with my regular shoes - Brooks. Don't buy They are not worth the money.

title: My shoes appear to be stuck in New York…
body: My shoes appear to be stuck in New York and since I didn’t receive them yet, it's difficult to give a review! They have been in New York for a week now.

title: trying to return 2 pairs of shoes
body: I have been trying to return 2 pairs of shoes I purchased the first week of April. I have not worn them I could not even get my foot in them, the backs are not sturdy and the shoe opening is so small I can't get my foot in them. First they wanted to send me a 30% refund, Then they want me to donate them to Salvation Army, get a receipt and send them the confirmation of my donation to them. ARE THEY GOING TO RETURN MY FULL PURCHASE PRICE OR ARE THEY GOING TO DEDUCT RETURN SHIPPING CHARGES EVEN THOUGH I DID NOT RETURN THEM??. THAT IS EVEN IF THEY DO SEND ME BACK A REFUND.

title: Response
body: Cannot get a response back

title: They run very small
body: I purchased a pair of Comfort Wear shoes. It took almost two weeks for them to arrive. I wear size 9 1/2 to 10 in shoes. The size I ordered was a 10. I could tell when I opened the bag (no box included) that they would be too small just by looking at them. They have a very strong chemical smell and as I expected they are too small. They look decent but unfortunately I am unable to wear them. What a waste of good money. If you are ordering them I'd get at least one size larger than you normally wear.

title: Ortho? SCAM! Don't Buy!
body: This company and this shoe is a play on the Ortho name and a scam. As other reviews have stated the customer service and return "policy" is fake/ non-existent. They took forever to arrive and finally did in a cheap bag. The shoes have no markings, sizes or labels on them whatsoever and appear to be manufactured for a couple of dollars. PLEASE save your money. Read the reviews and purchase an actual "brand" from a "real" company that stands behind their product with "real" customer service.

title: Review/Refund
body: I like the look of the shoe. It took more than 24-48 hrs to receive. When I received them it was a slip on which I dont wear. When I tried to return, because it was a promotion? I was denied getting money refunded/returned. My sister ordered the same shoe. She had a problem with the fit as well; she's a nurse and couldnt get her money back. Please do not purchase promotional shoes from these people, you will not receive a refund.

title: Unable to return or exchange, too large
body: I received the shoes I ordered but they were too large and the website says free returns or exchange. I've been trying to return or exchange for a smaller size with no luck. The response is slow and they want to let me keep the shoes but I can't figure out how to get a smaller size. I do not recommend this vendor.

title: Don’t order
body: I ordered these sneakers in hopes they would help with my foot pain. They were comfortable and give me the support I needed. But after wearing them, my feet hurt more than ever and feel like I have worked a 12 hour shift at the Waffle House. Definitely not recommending them to anyone

title: First time ordering a pair of shoes
body:
First time ordering a pair of shoes. The shoes had no support. Started the process of return by email but
they don't want the shoes back They offered a partial refund or take shoes to a Salvation Army or donation center, get a receipt and send the receipt to Comfortwear. Will never order again from this company.

title: Awesome customer service, uncomfortable shoes
body: I ordered a pair in a size 10. When they arrived I found out they were too small. I called the customer service number included with the shoes and was told to give these shoes to someone else and they’d send me another pair one size larger. They told me they’d only throw them away if I sent them back. Awesome customer service! Unfortunately, when the new shoes arrived they were the most uncomfortable shoes I’ve ever worn. I even tried 2 different kinds of insoles. No luck.

title: The shoes are hard to get on
body: The shoes are hard to get on, hard to wear. They come untied all the time. My feet slide to the front of the show and crush my toes. I gave them a chance to "break in" but they aren't getting much better. I'm not going to blame myself but something is very wrong here. Why are there not more honest reviews? Does the company delete bad reviews?

title: I just got these shoes yesterday
body: I just got these shoes yesterday, when I was home they seemed comfortable so I wore them to work. Thank God that I brought my old shoes with me. I am a nurse on my feet most of the night and these shoes don't have the support that I need. I having pain in my feet and lower back.

title: The shoes are comfortable but the…
body:
The shoes are comfortable but the opening at the shoe neck is too tight making it impossible to wear. And the return process is lengthy and complicated. At the end they didn’t refund all my money only 30% of it I gave the shoes to someone else hoping they can use them. Not recommended

Regard

title: Difficult
body: Very difficult to out on because of sock top and heel is too high and shoves your foot front.

title: Do not buy from this company
body: Do not buy from this company, they don’t honour their money back guarantee, only offering 30% refund or replacement shoes of choice, if you choose this option they send you the wrong size,, and then they do not respond to emails anymore…beware

title: Refund policy - don't believe it
body:
Return process - with some shoes if they are slip-ons, I cannot get my foot in the shoe. So I contacted them and they gave me 2 options for returning.
1. Keep the shoes and give them to someone else and get a 30% return.
2. Keep the shoes and we will give you another pair for free.
I don't want either as there return policy states they will give a full refund.

title: I just got the shoes and i'm very unhappy in the quality and the claims they made aren't true.
body: I just got the shoes and i'm very disappointed. They say that they're supposed to have an arch, they don't. They definitely don't fit correctly.According to the united states shoe sizes. They do not have enough support, my foot just twisted when I put it in the shoe. Very unhappy, spend the extra money and get Hoka, they are worth the money, these are not!

title: Not that great
body: When I first ordered I asked for black shoes because that’s the color we have to wear at work. Looking over the order, saw they were showing purple & black so contacted them and the assured me that they were changing the color because I had let them know in time. What did I get? Purple & black. And they didn’t fit right. Contacted them again and they rectified the problem by sending me black ones that fit. But I kept waiting for my feet to feel better and instead they seemed to hurt just as much as before. Then a friend recommended some inserts. Bought them and switched them out for the flimsy ones in these shoes and have been walking on air ever since. So bottom line, comfort wear needs to improve the inserts.

title: Beware! No free return.
body:
I purchased a pair after watching a FB ad, but I should have done my homework first.

There is no such thing as a free return or refund with these guys, despite their advertising. They only offer a partial return (30%) or to send another pair of shoes (from China). The shoes are extremely low quality with no arch support, once again, despite their adversiting. Needless to say, the shoes didn't fit nor met my needs despite being the right size.

As I didn't want to start a fight and risk being ghosted completely by the company, I settled for a partial return. I donated the shoes to a relative who can wear them with an extra insert for support.

Although their service rep Andrea was courteous and quick to respond to my emails (hence why I gave 2 stars instead of 0), I should have known better than to fall for this company's false advertising and waste my money. No more shoe shopping online for me.

title: I put my new shoes on this morning for…
body: I put my new shoes on this morning for the first time. The shoes are extremely attractive and not too difficult to get on. However, there doesn't seem to be very little or not any padding in them. No way I could wear them without socks and even with socks on, it is not very comfortable.

title: Don’t waste your money & time with this company
body: I believe all the reviews on their site must be a scam. These are not the best quality of shoes. I ordered my usual size 7 1/5 shoes size and as soon as I opened the package I knew they were going to be too small. When I contacted them for a return I was told I could keep or donate them and they would send me another pair in a different size or I could receive 30% of my money back. I took the getting another pair option because I spent $60 on them and didn’t want just 30% of my money back. So I received a new pair the next size up and my feet slide forward in them. Very disappointed in these shoes and the plastic cap on one of the laces already came off. I haven’t even worn them 1 hour yet. Plus I googled these shoes and others are selling them on Amazon for half the price! Do yourself a favor and if you’re considering buying these shoes buy them off Amazon for half the price and at least with Amazon you can return things without the hassle. After choosing getting another pair sent to me I’m stuck with 2 pairs of crappy shoes. I ended up getting a pair of comfy shoes that I use for my job where I stand on my feet for 8 hours at Marshall’s for $30. Companies pay for reviews and I’m wondering if that’s what this company did. I’m on Upwork and job after job posting is for reviews where you get money or free product for the review.

title: I ordered the shoes for my daughter to…
body:
I ordered the shoes for my daughter to use in her job. They were what she needed. Only complaint is the length of time it took to arrive.
My daughter says they are too big so she will have to return them for a smaller size.
I have asked for a return as per their policy but they are only offering another pair of shoes for half the price. This is not acceptable as the first pair is of no use. If I agree and purchase a second pair and it doesn’t fit then I am stuck with two pairs of shoes that don’t fit. This company is is not living up to its return policy. Buyer beware.

title: Refused refund
body:
It took over a . month to receive the shoes which did not fit. I requested to return them. They offered me a 30% refund of my $99.00 purchase or they would exchange 1 pair (a 50% refund since I ordered two shoes). All communication from customer service was aimed at emotionally manipulating me to keeping the shoes I could not use.

"It makes me really sad that you don't want a free pair offer."

"I don't want you to go through the hassle of paying to ship it back to us. Although this decision may be a loss for us but we value you as our customer."
I fully expect the refund to either take forever or never be given. If they are not equipped to deal with returns and refunds then they shouldn't be in this type of business.

title: Disappointed
body: I gave two stars due to when I contacted customer service regarding the shoes were too large; I was given a choice of taking a 30% discount and a pr of compression stockings or keep the shoes and receive another pair of shoes, no choice for a full refund since they were sending a second pair I have no option for a refund, which I was not given in the first place. I cannot wear the large shoes and I received the second pair and they bother my toes, my foot slides into the tip of the shoe; they're not too large, I believe it is the way the slope of the tips of the shoes are made so now I am stuck with two pairs of shoes I will not be able to wear and I am out of the money I spent. Disappointing, was looking forward to a comfortable pair of shoes to wear at work. It's extremely hard to find a good fitting pair of shoes. The customer service representative was very nice, I think that is just their policy and I even purchased the no hassle return guarantee.

title: Uncomfortable comfortwear shoes
body: Unfortunately these ComfortWear shoes were not comfortable on me. I wanted to return them. They offered me 50% back and give the shoes to someone else. This was unacceptable to me. They finally sent me a shipping label and I am sending them back for my total refund. This process was not as easy as stated on their website.

title: Save your money
body:
Save your money. These are not orthotic. They have a raised partial heal cushion, but that makes lots of pressure on toes and ball of foot. No arch support at all and material is very flexible, so not only is there no arch support. There is no foot support.
I will try them with orthotic insoles maybe. The return policy is that they don’t refund S&H and you have to pay shipping to return. They come from China, and some post offices won’t insure returns to China. Definitely not worth it!

title: These shoes are super excellent
body: These shoes are super excellent, one problem is that there is no size 12 and half American

title: POOR CUSTOMER SUPPORT
body: this company tells you to email them if the shoes need to be exchanged due to wrong size. I have been emailing them but i get no instructions or response on how I can exchange my shoes for the right size. VERY VERY POOR CUSTOMER SUPPORT. DO NOT ORDER FROM THIS COMPANY AND SAVE YOURSELF A HEARTACHE....

title: Buyer beware
body:
Places an order. Within 10min, got 6 emails confirming my order. Then, I got 2 more telling me my order was "interrupted"? I looked up the contact info, and it gave a phone number and email form. You can't speak to a human on the phone, and email replies take 24-48 hours. $$ we taken in my account, but the emails said it was interrupted? When I ran the order number, it said to contact customer service?
If I spend hundreds on a product, I want to talk to a human being, not wait 2 days to get a reply

title: I sent an email to the email listed on…
body:
I sent an email to the email listed on the order confirmation. I wanted to return my shoes. After 3 emails I finally was given a return address.

The person I was in communication with was always 'sad' my shoes hurt my feet and kept asking me to keep the shoes, wear them and hopefully me feet would stop hurting.

They are too small in size and width. My arches have dropped making the arch support too much. Where it was causing the bridge of my foot pain. These 3 reasons were all explained in my emails. Yet this person was still refusing to give me a return label.

Finally I was given a address to which I need to pay for shipping. I need to provide proof I have also shipped them. And I 'might' get a full refund.

This was NOT hassle free.

I have to say the shoes are super light weight. And think they could work for the right type of foot.

Fingers crossed I get a FULL refund.

title: No slip shoes.
body:
Thank you, ComfortWear! I bought two pairs of shoes and they are no slip shoes. Sorry but one of the pairs really hurt my foot while at work.
The other pair of shoes I bought from ComfortWear work out great! Thank you so much for the opportunity to buy them!

title: Not nursing shoes. False advertising.
body:
Easy to buy and postage was fast but unfortunately the shoes aren't what I thought they would be, going on what was advertised.
I've been a midwife for 18 years, working 12.5hrs shifts, plus overtime, sometimes up to 80-110 hours a week. I've spent a LOT of money on shoes in the past but only ever found one pair that were worth it.
I purchased these because they were advertised as orthotic and supportive with good shock absorption on the soles.
Unfortunately the first thing I noticed when I opened the packet was the top is canvas, which didn't show in the pics. This makes them useless for nursing and should be addressed immediately or the company must stop the false advertising toward nurses/midwives immediately. Medical professionals MUST wear full upper leather shoes. It's an OH+S issue for, we need to protect ourselves and the people we care for.
Secondly canvas is not supportive at all and would not be wearable for the long hours I do on my feet constantly. I've worn them a few times at home to see if I can make use of them but I can't. And, finally the base is not as soft as I thought it would be for the price. Absolutely overpriced, ridiculously overpriced for what they are, and I can't wear them at work anyway, so now I have to find another pair that I CAN wear at work. It's all a total bother and exhausting and I'm truly very tired and fed up. I just wanted something simple. I will need a refund. I am happy to post the shoes back if supplied with a return pre paid sticker.

title: There are better shoes/companies out there so try to avoid this one.
body: First of all, I paid extra for expedited shipping and the shoes took almost 1 month to arrive. They communicated and apologized for the delay and they offered a discount on my next purchase, but would not refund the amount that i paid for faster shipping. The tops of the shoes are comfortable I guess but the soles are NOT! The bottom of my feet are very sore at the end of the day. The site states that they run small so to order 1 size up. I did and they don't fit. They are too big. I know after I contact them, send a bunch of pictures, and then PAY to ship them back, I could get a replacement pair. After they receive the old pair of course. So, I would essentially be waiting 2 months for my new pair that i spent even more money on which may or may not fit and are not great shoes. So I put some insoles inside to try to fill the extra space. We'll see how that goes. I learned to just stick with companies in the USA that offer free, easy, and quick returns because Comfort Wear is not the company for that.

title: Shoes run large
body: While the shoes have great support underneath, they have no support on the sides. The sides are like a sock type of material. Also, these shoes run large. The arch support is too far forward or isn't enough of an arch support at all. I would not purchase again.

title: Still getting used to them
body: Still getting used to them. They feel hard when you walk. The soles of the shoe’s are hard. Hoping after wearing them, they will loosen up. Love the roominess of the shoes.

title: THE SHOES ARE REALLY COMFY
body: THE SHOES ARE REALLY COMFY. ONE PAIR IS PERFECT THE OTHER IS ACTUALLY TO SLOPPY. BOTH SAME SIZE. I WILL JUST WEAR A HEAVIER SOCK FOR THE SLOPPY ONE. THEY LOOK AND FEEL WONDERFUL.

title: Just got them! Not sure about them!
body:
First of all they come from china! So delivery is longer than you would expect. I wear an 8.5, these are a little shorter. Should have ordered a nine. I can feel places where my toes fit. Ok, that might be a good thing. I feel no cushioning in the foot bed. They feel comfortable as long as you're sitting. Walking is uncomfortable.
I am surprized by other reviews stating they are too big. Trying to give it a couple of days to get use to.

title: The Good, Bad, and Ugly
body: Customer service was amazing. After getting the shoes and wearing them twice to work on the concrete floors, after 2 hours, my feet hurt so bad that I had to go out to my car and put on my old cheap department store shoes on. New shoes felt like I was walking on hard grated trays, and made my feet hurt so bad, I could have cried. Tops of feet were great, but bottoms of feet were miserable.

title: It took quite a while to get the…
body: It took quite a while to get the product, but once it arrived, it was neatly packaged, and the shoe looks excellent. I had called in advanced and spoken to someone asking if they could accommodate narrow feet and I was promised they could. The shoe is too wide.

title: Still not sure these will perform as…
body: Still not sure these will perform as well as they claim The shoes are wide and if you have a narrow foot you’ll need to wear heavy socks. Be careful when ordering got the compression socks as I didn’t see how to not get them. Remember there are no refunds.

title: My feet are still crying a day later. Totally truthful!
body: The shoes arrived in 2 weeks. Thats not bad. I wore them shopping as soon as I recieved them. You said to be honest. I had the worst 4hrs of shopping that I ever had. My feet were killing me after 1 hour. We had to stop and sit for awhile so we ate dinner. I want to return for a refund. They had no support for my plantarfacitis. Im totally disappointed. The shoes are very nice looking but they arent at all supporting.

title: Shipping was very slow and not very…
body: Shipping was very slow and not very timely. Also shoes went on better sale price hours after my purchase which I feel there should’ve been some kind of a refund for difference in price.

title: Very disappointing
body: So, I see all these ads for these shoes, and it worked, i took the bait. With that said, i did not order from tiktok shop or any social link. I went directly to their website. I have so many feet issues, and as always, hoping this latest thing, is THE one. Its not. As a matter of fact, i have had better luck with shoes bought at Costco. so i ordered, waited, waited, received lots of emails for more specials, etc. So it felt like forever, so i reached out and was starting to feel that i had fallen for a scam. I will say, the got right back to me, with tracking information etc. Shoes took a wee bit over 2 weeks to arrive. they are very nice looking, I really like the way they looked. Next day, put them on, and looked forward to a good day. Oh my, my feet were killing me before half way through the day, and I wasn't even on my feet that much, fairly normal amount, but its not like i was standing or walking for 8 hours straight. the bottoms felt like i was walking on bricks, literally no padding / cushion whatever. I haven't worn them since. I will try them again, but definitely only when i know that i won't be on my feet or walking a lot. They look great, they slide on great, as a matter of fact when i first put them on, I was amazed, they felt a little weird, but in a good way, but within a couple of hours, i was very sorry i chose those shoes. I have not tried to return them so i have no idea if they stand behind their policy. at this point, not that i have money to burn, I just don't have the time or the energy, so win for them, but I will not be ordering more, and these will last me a very long time since i wont be wearing them very often.

title: I think the shoes I ordered are a…
body: I think the shoes I ordered are a little to big it said size up so I did worn only once

title: I got the wrong size
body: I got the wrong size, no time for me to send it back, need it now. Took long to get here.

title: I love my shoes ,,,
body: I love my shoes ,,,, just a little big though !

title: The jury is still out on these shoes
body: The shoes are comfortable, but I feel like I'm walking on my tip toes. The heel seems higher than the toe of the shoe and takes some getting used to. Also, the insole slips and bunches up. I'm still deciding whether they will work for me or not. I want them to!

title: They are comfortable but hard to put…
body: They are comfortable but hard to put on. The arch seems to fall under my heel rather than my actual arch. The shoe fits otherwise. Also the sandle seems to run small as I got the same as my shoe but just a little small

title: Non-Slip Isnt So Non-Slip…
body: I think the non-slip soles could be made better. Wore them to work twice now. I've had a few times in the kitchen my foot has slid. I don't see myself wearing these more they a couple months. Try to get some of my $'s worth. I'll go back to buying Shoes For Crews or Sketchers

title: The shoes does not display actual size
body:
The shoes does not display actual size. I ordered half size larger since I was not able to try them ; fit comfortably but hurt my toes… and the apparatus at the end of the lace has fallen off.
You used an account that was not authorized… an account of which I was considering using prior to my actual purchase which was from another account.

title: Shipping took longer than expected
body:
Shipping took longer than expected.
No ankle support and size was larger than usual.

title: Disappointed
body:
Claiming run small but untrue. Ordered 1/2 size bigger but almost too big. Fairly comfortable but Not anywhere in the
league of Vionics. Was so excited to get but disappointed once they came in.

title: These shoes are comfortable butnot
body: These shoes are comfortable but not meant for standing on your feet or walking on concrete for hours. The cushion is all the heel and after about 3hours the front part of my feet were absolutely feeling the discomfort. They are cute and the tops feel like you have really cool socks on a shoe base

title: Disappointed on 1 out of 3 pairs purchased
body: I bought 3 pairs of shoes from ComfortWear for my Plantar Fasciiatis. I liked the Stretch Cushion shoes and the Arch Support sandals but was very disappointed with the quality of the Roman Cushion sandal. The bottom soles are made out of hard plastic instead of a slip resistant outsole. Not much cushion either. This sandal was definitely not worth buying.

title: No cushion feels like I am walking on…
body: No cushion feels like I am walking on the floor. I thought they were going to be comfortable I lasted 1 hr in them

title: Ordering was very easy & efficient but…
body: Ordering was very easy & efficient but shipping seems to be taking a lot longer than I thought. I ordered over 10 days ago!! No information on where my package was or is. I won’t order again!!!

title: Coming from China & will take at least 2 weeks to receive!!
body:
I have not received my 2 pairs of shoes but, they are scheduled for delivery in two days. If they arrive on Friday, it will have taken them 12 days in transit and 2 days in processing prior to shipping for a total of 14 days to receive my order. My complaint is, nowhere on the product website or during the checkout process does it tell you the product is shipping from China. After you have made your purchase, you get an email saying it may take 8-12 days for shipping from China. I also had to pay international payment fee to my bank. I sent an email immediately after receiving the confirmation email informing me of the country of origin & the shipping time to cancel the order. I received an email stating it could not be cancelled since it was in processing for shipping. These 3 emails were sent and delivered within 1 hour of check out. It was 2 days when I received an email stating processing was complete and package was being processed for shipping. Why couldn’t my order be cancelled over the 2 days of processing?
I am now concerned what will happen if my son’s pair shoes &/or my pair of shoes do not fit therefore, needing to be returned.

It does say full refund and that is why I ordered them. I asked them for some inserts to try and make them more comfortable but they said no, and still would not take them back. So they offered me a new pair, different style. I picked the other style they showed but when they showed up they were the same style but different color. I then wrote to them again stating they sent me the wrong style. ComfortWear told me since I accepted the free pair there was nothing else they could do. Very disappointing.

body: I fail to see what all the hype about these shoes are. I received my pair and they are cheaply made, the toe box is too narrow, the comfort sole seems to only be in half of the shoe.

title: Order down 1/2 size
body:
The website says to order up a half size because shoes run small. I wear a 7 so I order 7 1/2. When they arrived they were huge. They did fit a friend who normally wears an 8 1/2. I measured my foot and according to their size chart I needed a 9!! Nope. So emailed customer support they answered promptly but initially wanted pictures, then another picture.
Then only offered me a 30% discount. The shoes were huge so no, I didn't want them, just an exchange. They stated free returns or exchanges and I only ordered because of that policy guarantee. So I emailed that I would like another size. They relented and finally agreed to send a replacement size and told me that I couldn't return these because THEY were going above and beyond. I was then concerned about what size to order since this was my "last chance" I ordered a 1/2 size smaller and I just received them and that seems to be correct. So far they feel very comfortable, wide toe area but I've only worn about an hour. I'd say get them but good luck on the size and pursue your "free return or exchange" because they won't offer that initially. As for the original pair they ended up telling me to keep them for hygienic reasons even though they were only tried on. So I'll donate the pair that didn't fit.

title: Comfortware Review-South Carolina
body: The sneakers are a bit pricey but I don't mind if the quality & comfort are worth it. First, the fit is too big but I've already worn them so not returning. You can easily go down a half size. I don't wear socks so I like to slide into my sneakers but the bottom of the inside sole comes up every single time. Very irritating. They have a great style look & aren't heavy, cloddy looking! If the fit were better (tighter) they would likely be perfect! :)

title: No Such Thing as a Free Return ** But They Did Send Another Pair at no charge
body: They advertise free returns and exchanges. Yet when the boots I ordered for my daughter proved to be too small, Danny's offer was to get an additional 30% off the boots and then get 20% off a new boot purchase. These folks are in China but want you to think they're right next door. They're not, and they don't take returns. Calculate that into your total cost IF you dare to order from these people. ** Fed 11 update: after several emails back and forth, Danny asked me to send a snip of the boot type and size I wanted, and he would send me the boot in the larger size. Three weeks later -- I received the larger boots, in the larger size. So I'm increasing the rating for these folks to 3 stars, but why they advertise returns when they don't take them is beyond me. Danny did make it right with the followup shipment.

It took awhile for the shoes to arrive.

The minute you put your foot in them, you can tell something is different. It reminded me a little of an orthotic. It adjusted the way my feet sat in the shoe and that took a little getting used to but they feel pretty supportive.

I went for a walk, worked around my home and did errands the first day. My feet weren’t in pain, cramped or tired after wearing them most of the day.

The sole is very sturdy, the fabric upper - is stretchy. My only concern would be that the upper might stretch out over time. But so far, so good. Haven’t had them long enough to determine that.
4 stars for that reason and delivery time.

title: Easy to order

title: They run a bit big.
body: I do like the shoes but I think they run a bit big. Not big enough to return them but I would order a half size smaller if I do another pair. They took a while to be delivered as it looked like they came from China! I thought this was an American company!

It’s a very light shoe hardly any substance to it and walking in. It is OK my plantar fasciitis didn’t hurt it but other things hurt my knees hurt other things hurt. I just wasn’t comfortable in it as I was hoping I would be. I don’t know if I them and I don’t think I would buy them again because I’m not really impressed but I could come back in three months and have a whole different review. This is just my first week of use and I’m not sure.

title: Prompt and efficient vendor

Thank you for the prompt and efficient shipping. I found ComfortWear to be very responsive to my communications.

title: Easy ordering process
